definition: The first of the five heaps: form considered as one member of the coordinated heap family.
notes: Keep this compound aligned with the headword defaults of rūpa and khandha. The project prefers form heap so the constituent members read in one voice with the collective expression five heaps. Aggregate of form remains a comparative alternate in explanatory prose, but it should not displace the house rendering in direct translation. SN 22.48 supports this canonical constituent-compound usage.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose using familiar aggregate terminology
context_rules.notes: Use this in explanatory prose only for orientation.

version_notes: Expanded to keep the constituent heap compounds aligned with the house heap-family defaults.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'form heap' as the default rendering for the compound unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not rotate to aggregate-language in direct translation by habit.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the constituent heap compound from drifting away from the coordinated five-heaps family.
